recommendation.issue_summary: Shipment SHP-008 is experiencing high risk, urgency, and impact due to various alerts and signals from different sources, including terminal, transport, warehouse, customs, and finance.

rationale_per_action_id.ACT-REROUTE-1: Rerouting via SGSIN -> INBOM [OCEAN] using carrier set CAR-01 has the highest score (95.59) and feasibility, with an eta delta of -6.96 hours and cost delta of $105.0.
rationale_per_action_id.ACT-REROUTE-3: Rerouting via SGSIN -> AEDXB -> INBOM [OCEAN->OCEAN] using carrier set CAR-04,CAR-04 has a score of 94.91 and feasibility, with an eta delta of -8.08 hours and cost delta of $127.5.
rationale_per_action_id.ACT-REROUTE-2: Rerouting via SGSIN -> INNSA -> INBOM [OCEAN->COASTAL] using carrier set CAR-02,CAR-02 has a score of 94.4 and feasibility, with an eta delta of -7.76 hours and cost delta of $120.0.
rationale_per_action_id.ACT-REROUTE-4: Rerouting via SGSIN -> NLRTM -> INBOM [OCEAN->OCEAN] using carrier set CAR-03,CAR-03 has a score of 92.03 and feasibility, with an eta delta of -24.56 hours and cost delta of $277.5.
rationale_per_action_id.ACT-SLOT-1: Expedite terminal slot at T-SGD1 has a score of 81.42 and feasibility, with an eta delta of -0.6 hours and cost delta of $150.0.

recommendation.expected_impact: The selected action is expected to reduce the risk, urgency, and impact of the shipment by rerouting it via SGSIN -> INBOM [OCEAN] using carrier set CAR-01.

recommendation.issue_summary: Shipment SHP-008 faces high risk of SLA breach (98%) and significant demurrage accrual ($3500) due to terminal congestion, gate bottlenecks, and customs inspection.

ACT-REROUTE-1: Situation: Shipment SHP-008 faces high SLA breach risk (98%) and $3500 demurrage due to rapid yard occupancy increase (6.6%/hr) and gate throughput degradation (20 trucks/hr).
Evidence: Yard occupancy rate of change (6.6%), gate throughput trucks per hr (20), vessel departure delay min (15), demurrage accrual usd (3500).
Constraint check: Customs cleared, feasible reroute options available.
Action items: 1) Reroute via SGSIN -> INBOM using CAR-01. 2) Verify CAR-01's vehicle availability. 3) Update shipment route and notify stakeholders.
Why this over alternatives: ACT-REROUTE-1 offers best eta delta (-6.96 hrs) and moderate cost delta ($105) with high feasibility.
ACT-REROUTE-2: Situation: Similar to ACT-REROUTE-1, but with different route and carrier.
Evidence: Delivery delay min (240), carrier reliability score (0.43), unassigned shipments count (12).
Constraint check: Feasible, but check for CAR-02 availability.
Action items: 1) Reroute via SGSIN -> INNSA -> INBOM using CAR-02,CAR-02. 2) Confirm CAR-02's capacity.
Why this over alternatives: ACT-REROUTE-2 provides eta delta (-7.76 hrs) with $120 cost delta.
ACT-REROUTE-3: Situation: Multi-carrier, multi-port reroute option.
Evidence: Load completion pct (68%), inventory readiness pct (76%).
Constraint check: Ensure CAR-04 availability and coordination.
Action items: 1) Reroute via SGSIN -> AEDXB -> INBOM using CAR-04,CAR-04. 2) Verify CAR-04's vehicle schedule.
Why this over alternatives: Offers eta delta (-8.08 hrs), but with higher cost delta ($127.5).
ACT-REROUTE-4: Situation: Longest eta delta but also highest cost.
Evidence: Time to SLA breach hrs (21), SLA breach probability pct (98%).
Constraint check: Verify feasibility with CAR-03 and long-term capacity.
Action items: 1) Reroute via SGSIN -> NLRTM -> INBOM using CAR-03,CAR-03. 2) Assess long-term benefits.
Why this over alternatives: Significant eta delta (-24.56 hrs) but with highest cost delta ($277.5).
ACT-SLOT-1: Situation: Expedite terminal slot for quicker processing.
Evidence: Gate queue depth (5), yard occupancy pct (51.7%).
Constraint check: Ensure T-SGD1 has available slot and CAR-02's agreement.
Action items: 1) Move shipment to earliest available slot at T-SGD1. 2) Confirm with terminal operator.
Why this over alternatives: Quick eta delta (-0.6 hrs) with $150 cost delta.

expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-1: Reduced SLA breach risk, lower demurrage accrual
expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-2: Similar to ACT-REROUTE-1 but with different route
expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-4: Significant eta improvement but higher cost
expected_impact.ACT-SLOT-1: Quick terminal processing with moderate cost

Customs is not yet cleared; dispatch requires hold gate

recommendation.issue_summary: Shipment SHP-010 faces delays due to customs clearance issues, terminal congestion, and potential SLA breach.

ACT-CUSTOMS-HOLD-1: Situation: Customs clearance is pending, and dispatch is delayed.
Evidence: customs_clearance_status = REJECTED, customs_document_completeness_pct = 80, inspection_flag = TRUE.
Constraint check: Since customs is not cleared, dispatch cannot proceed. This action aligns with the constraint to prioritize customs resolution.
Action items: 1) Expedite customs resolution packet. 2) Hold dispatch until customs clearance is obtained.
Why this over alternatives: This action directly addresses the immediate constraint of customs clearance, making it a prerequisite for other actions like rerouting or slot changes.
ACT-SLOT-1: Situation: Terminal congestion is affecting shipment processing.
Evidence: yard_occupancy_pct = 59.3, gate_queue_depth = 41, crane_plan_execution_pct = 52.7.
Constraint check: This action does not conflict with customs clearance status but may not fully address the congestion issue.
Action items: 1) Move shipment to the earliest available terminal slot at T-SGD1.
Why this over alternatives: This provides a local mitigation strategy for terminal congestion but does not address the customs clearance issue.
ACT-REROUTE-1: Situation: Rerouting may alleviate some delays but requires customs clearance.
Evidence: eta_delta_hours = -7.33, cost_delta_usd = 105.
Constraint check: This action is blocked due to customs not being cleared.
Action items: 1) Wait for customs clearance. 2) Consider rerouting if clearance is obtained.
Why this over alternatives: This offers a potential long-term solution but is currently infeasible due to customs constraints.
ACT-REROUTE-2: Situation: Similar to ACT-REROUTE-1, this requires customs clearance.
Evidence: eta_delta_hours = -8.13, cost_delta_usd = 120.
Constraint check: Also blocked due to customs clearance issues.
Action items: Same as ACT-REROUTE-1.
Why this over alternatives: Offers another rerouting option but with different cost and ETA implications.
ACT-REROUTE-3: Situation: Another rerouting option with similar constraints.
Evidence: eta_delta_hours = -8.46, cost_delta_usd = 127.5.
Constraint check: Blocked for the same reasons.
Action items: Same as ACT-REROUTE-1.
Why this over alternatives: Provides a slightly different route with cost and ETA trade-offs.
ACT-REROUTE-4: Situation: A more drastic rerouting option.
Why this over alternatives: Offers significant ETA improvement but at a higher cost and currently infeasible.

expected_impact.ACT-CUSTOMS-HOLD-1: Immediate resolution of customs issues to enable dispatch.
expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-1: Potential ETA reduction and cost savings if customs clearance is obtained.
expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-2: Similar to ACT-REROUTE-1 but with different trade-offs.
expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-3: Another option for rerouting with specific benefits.
expected_impact.ACT-REROUTE-4: Significant ETA improvement at higher cost.

recommendation.issue_summary: SHP-002 is blocked at T-RTM1: customs docs 50% incomplete, clearance REJECTED after 103h, yard occupancy rising 8.5%/hr, crane down 45min, load only 59% complete, driver HOS 1.7h left, SLA breach 95% in 60h.

ACT-CUSTOMS-HOLD-1: Situation: Container C-1001 cannot gate-out because customs clearance is REJECTED and documents only 50% complete.
Evidence: customs_clearance_status=REJECTED, clearance_duration_hrs=103, document_completeness_pct=50, demurrage_accrual_usd=0, free_time_expiry_hrs_remaining=29.
Constraint check: customs not CLEARED → reroute/slot actions blocked; ACT-CUSTOMS-HOLD-1 feasible and unblocked.

Action items: 1) Notify broker to complete missing docs within 2h. 2) Reserve hold gate slot to keep container inside yard. 3) Escalate to customs supervisor for priority review. 4) Update TMS to prevent dispatch until status=CLEARED.
Why this over alternatives: reroutes (ACT-REROTE-2/1/3) and slot expedite (ACT-SLOT-1) are blocked by same customs constraint; customs resolution is the only action that removes the root block.
ACT-SLOT-1: Situation: Yard occupancy 55.1% rising 8.5%/hr; earliest slot move could avoid congestion.
Evidence: yard_occupancy_pct=55.1, yard_occupancy_rate_of_change=8.5, gate_queue_depth=9, crane_downtime_min=45, crane_plan_execution_pct=55.
Constraint check: customs not CLEARED → dispatch still blocked even if slot moved; feasibility true but dependency on customs remains.

Action items: 1) Coordinate with T-RTM1 slot desk to secure next available window. 2) Hold container in designated buffer lane until docs cleared. 3) Monitor yard rate; if >70% occupancy, trigger yard-closure protocol.
Why this over alternatives: gives local congestion relief (-0.61h) but cannot proceed to vessel until customs fixed; lower score than customs fix.
ACT-REROUTE-2: Situation: Ocean route NLRTM->AEDXB->INBOM offers 11.74h earlier ETA once container is customs-cleared.
Evidence: eta_delta_hours=-11.74, cost_delta_usd=162.5, carrier_id=CAR-04, blocked_reasons=['Customs is not yet cleared; dispatch requires hold gate'].
Constraint check: customs not CLEARED → blocked; feasibility true but execution gated.
Action items: 1) Keep reroute booking on hold. 2) After customs=CLEARED, release new manifest to carrier CAR-04. 3) Cancel original vessel slot to avoid no-show fee.
Why this over alternatives: best ETA gain among reroutes but still secondary to customs clearance; cost higher than ACT-REROUTE-1.
ACT-REROUTE-1: Situation: Direct NLRTM->INBOM ocean leg with CAR-03 saves 11.74h and costs 135 USD.
Evidence: eta_delta_hours=-11.74, cost_delta_usd=135, carrier_reliability_score=0.67, blocked_reasons=['Customs is not yet cleared; dispatch requires hold gate'].
Constraint check: customs not CLEARED → blocked; feasibility true.
Action items: 1) Pre-book CAR-03 slot contingent on customs clearance within 24h. 2) Monitor carrier reliability; if score drops <0.6, switch to ACT-REROUTE-2.
Why this over alternatives: cheapest reroute option but same customs dependency; score slightly lower than ACT-REROUTE-2 due to carrier reliability.
ACT-REROUTE-3: Situation: Coastal leg via INNSA gives largest ETA improvement (-12.22h) but extra transhipment.
Evidence: eta_delta_hours=-12.22, cost_delta_usd=146, transhipment_missed_flag=FALSE, blocked_reasons=['Customs is not yet cleared; dispatch requires hold gate'].
Constraint check: customs not CLEARED → blocked; feasibility true.
Action items: 1) Secure both ocean and coastal bookings. 2) Validate INNSA yard capacity for tranship window. 3) After customs clearance, release container to first leg.
Why this over alternatives: greatest time saving but marginally higher cost and complexity; still gated by customs.

triggered_rules: No dispatch when customs status ≠ CLEARED
Customs resolution preferred before reroute when clearance blocked
Local congestion mitigation acceptable only after root block removed
recommendation.expected_impact: Completing customs docs and obtaining clearance unblocks entire chain: container can gate-out, catch next vessel, reduce SLA breach probability from 95% to <30%, and avoid demurrage once free time expires in 29h.
